mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month rise in FEDFUNDS reflects an active Fed tightening path that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days. As of the snapshot FEDFUNDS stands at 2.33% and has been rising steadily since March 2022.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose from near-zero in early 2022 to 2.33% by August 2022
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX posts positive 20d returns in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS rose vs prior month.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds an elevated threshold, markets price a higher probability of aggressive Fed rate hikes, causing an immediate repricing shock in equities over the next 5 trading days. CPIAUCSL at 295.62 implies inflation remains well above the Fed's 2% target as of the snapshot.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL level 295.62 as of one month prior to decision date, consistent with high YoY inflation readings through mid-2022
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 8%, forward 5d SPX returns are positive on average or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across at least 10 triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising FEDFUNDS and historically low UNRATE indicates a labor market tight enough to sustain wage-driven inflation, reinforcing the Fed's resolve to keep hiking and depressing equity valuations over a 60-day horizon. Current values show FEDFUNDS at 2.33% rising alongside UNRATE at 3.7%, near multi-decade lows.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% while FEDFUNDS has risen for six consecutive months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC of the FEDFUNDS-UNRATE interaction term with forward 60d returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction signal underperforms the single-series FEDFUNDS trend signal in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a Fed tightening cycle, declines in HOUST (housing starts) signal that higher mortgage rates are suppressing rate-sensitive demand, a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. HOUST is 1446k units, down from 2022 highs as FEDFUNDS has climbed.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1446k as of two months prior to decision date, declining trend since early 2022
failure_condition: HOUST decline fails to precede negative SPX 20d returns in more than 55% of tightening-cycle episodes historically, or the relationship reverses sign outside tightening regimes without being flagged as regime-conditional.

mechanism_narrative: Continued monthly gains in PAYEMS signal resilient labor demand and consumer income support, sustaining corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as recession fears are deferred. PAYEMS stands at 152,744 thousand, still expanding through August 2022.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has posted consecutive monthly increases through the 2022 snapshot
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS momentum fails to correlate with positive forward 20d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any rolling 2-year window, or negative returns occur in >50% of months with rising PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A two-consecutive-month decline in INDPRO signals contracting industrial output and an emerging supply-side slowdown, triggering a rapid negative repricing of equities within 5 trading days as growth expectations deteriorate. INDPRO reads 104.55 at the snapshot.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO level 104.5464 as of one month prior to decision date
failure_condition: Two-month INDPRO declines are not followed by negative forward 5d SPX returns in at least 55% of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in RSAFS growth signals weakening consumer spending, which under a high-inflation, high-rate regime foreshadows compressed corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon, as consumers face eroded real purchasing power. RSAFS is 683,291 million dollars at the snapshot.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS nominal growth decelerating relative to CPIAUCSL inflation, implying weaker real retail sales growth
failure_condition: Real RSAFS growth deceleration (nominal RSAFS growth minus CPIAUCSL inflation) fails to precede negative 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of historical high-inflation episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a rising UNRATE and decelerating PAYEMS growth in a late-cycle tightening environment signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration, which precedes broader earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. Currently UNRATE is low at 3.7% and PAYEMS growth remains positive, so this signal is not yet active but is monitored for regime shift.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months while PAYEMS monthly change turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% and PAYEMS still expanding as of the snapshot, with FEDFUNDS in an active tightening cycle
failure_condition: Following joint UNRATE-rise/PAYEMS-decline triggers, forward 60d SPX returns are positive on average or not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across at least 8 historical triggered episodes.
